title: thymus longiflorus subsp. ciliatus
intro: Thymus longiflorus subsp. ciliatus, commonly known as the ciliated thyme, is a subspecies of the thymus longiflorus plant, a type of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils such as thymol and carvacrol. The health benefits of T. longiflorus subsp. ciliatus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens. Thymus longiflorus subsp. ciliatus can be consumed as a tea, used as a topical application, or incorporated into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and essential oils.
